name: Mézinais – Ténarèze Circuit
name_original: Eauze, Courrensan et Fourcès – boucle dans le Pays d'Armagnac
description: This difficult 45.3-mile touring cycling loop through Armagnac Country features historic villages like Fourcès and significant elevation gai
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Gondrin traverse a slightly hilly Gascon landscape, characterized by expansive Armagnac vineyards and quiet country roads. The terrain offers a mix of engaging routes, from gentle gradients along greenways to more challenging paths with moderate climbs. This region in Gers, France, provides diverse cycling experiences amidst picturesque scenery and historical sites. Best touring cycling routes around Gondrin The most popular touring cycling route is Eauze: Place d'Armagnac – Eauze loop from Gondrin, a 21.0 miles (33.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route leads to Eauze, known as the Armagnac capital, and is particularly appealing on market days. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fourcès, the round village – Fourcès loop from Gondrin, a moderate 24.6 miles (39.6 km) path. This route explores charming medieval bastides and offers views over the Armagnac vineyards on quiet roads. Local touring cyclists also love the Condom – Armagnac Greenway loop from Gondrin, a 54.2 miles (87.2 km) trail leading through the "Voie Verte" (Greenway), often completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes. This path, built on an old railway track, offers a shaded cycling experience with a gentle gradient. Touring cycling around Gondrin is defined by hilly Gascon landscapes, Armagnac vineyards, and medieval bastides. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Gondrin are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gondrin's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Gondrin?
A: The touring cycling routes around Gondrin traverse a slightly hilly Gascon landscape. You'll find yourself cycling through expansive Armagnac vineyards on quiet country roads, offering a mix of engaging routes from gentle gradients to more challenging paths with moderate climbs.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Gondrin offers routes with varying difficulty levels. For an easier ride, consider the "Voie Verte" (Greenway) which is built on an old railway track, providing a shaded cycling experience with a gentle gradient. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41610998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Condom – Armagnac Greenway loop from Gondrin&lt;/a&gt; incorporates this path.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history. Routes often lead to charming medieval bastides like Fourcès and Montréal-du-Gers. You can also pass by impressive medieval churches and castles, such as the remains of Chateau Lagardère. Notable nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3770084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larressingle Fortified Village&lt;/a&gt; and the former Cistercian &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7076323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flaran Abbey&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Gondrin?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Gondrin are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20638674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fourcès, the round village – Fourcès loop from Gondrin&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 39.6 km path that explores charming medieval bastides. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021108660/mezinais-tenareze-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mézinais – Ténarèze Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, a more challenging 72.8 km loop.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Gondrin?
A: The touring cycling routes in Gondrin are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque scenery, the quiet country roads, and the diverse experiences offered, from vineyard tours to historical village explorations.

Q: Can I find places to explore or visit in the villages along the routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes lead to charming villages and towns. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7012871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eauze: Place d'Armagnac – Eauze loop from Gondrin&lt;/a&gt; takes you to Eauze, the Armagnac capital, which is particularly lively on market days. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3908601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montréal (Gers) Village&lt;/a&gt;, known for its medieval charm.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by traditional Gascon architecture?
A: Yes, some tours venture south from Gondrin, showcasing beautiful traditional Gascon mansions built from local stone. These routes offer a glimpse into the region's unique architectural heritage amidst the rolling countryside.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available from Gondrin?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41610998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Condom – Armagnac Greenway loop from Gondrin&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 87.2 km. This route is often completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes and offers a substantial ride through the scenic Armagnac region.

Q: Are there any routes that include castles or fortified structures?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with historical fortifications. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roques – Lagardère Castle loop from Betbèze&lt;/a&gt; will take you past the remains of Chateau Lagardère. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3770084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larressingle Fortified Village&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent historical site accessible via some routes.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on the touring cycling routes?
A: The most prominent natural features are the expansive Armagnac vineyards that dominate the rolling, green landscape of the Gers. You'll also experience the region's slightly hilly terrain, offering picturesque views and a refreshing natural environment for your ride.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in total around Gondrin?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Gondrin, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 5 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Can I find routes that lead to other towns or cities in the region?
A: Yes, several routes connect Gondrin to other notable towns. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7012871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eauze: Place d'Armagnac – Eauze loop from Gondrin&lt;/a&gt; leads to Eauze, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41610998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Condom – Armagnac Greenway loop from Gondrin&lt;/a&gt; takes you towards Condom, offering opportunities to explore beyond Gondrin itself.
tips: Very old Castelnau-type village overlooking the Auzoue from a rocky outcrop. Impressive private feudal castle which can be walked around by the path of the gardens.

The town of Eauze has a real archaeological treasure dating from Roman times. A treasure buried in the 3rd century of the Roman Empire on the site of a Roman villa of notables from the ancient city of Elusa. It can be seen at the city's archaeological museum. 28,054 coins, jewelry and precious objects have been found in excavations dating back to 1985.
Village all in circles around its green square.

faqs.question: Is this cycling loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is categorized as a "difficult" bike ride and requires "very good fitness" due to its distance of 72.8 kilometers and an elevation gain of 690 meters. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Eauze, Courrensan and Fourcès loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly paved, consisting of rolling country roads that wind through vast vineyards and bright yellow sunflower fields. While generally picturesque, the significant elevation gain means you'll encounter challenging climbs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several charming and historic locations. Eauze, the 'Capitol of the Armagnac,' offers Roman archaeological treasures. Courrensan is a very old Castelnau-type village with an impressive private feudal castle. The highlight is often Fourcès, a magnificent round medieval bastide unique for its circular central square.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best times are typically spring (April-May) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is pleasant for cycling. Summer can be beautiful, especially with the sunflower fields in bloom, but temperatures can be quite high, making the climbs more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the cycling loop?
faqs.answer: As this is a loop trail, you can conveniently start and park in any of the main towns along the route. Good options include Eauze, Courrensan, or Fourcès, where you'll find public parking areas suitable for starting your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Eauze, Courrensan and Fourcès loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces in France, cycling with a dog on this particular route would be challenging. It's a long, difficult road cycling route with significant traffic in some areas. If you wish to bring your dog, a bicycle trailer would be necessary, but consider the strenuous nature of the ride for both you and your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this loop. It primarily uses public roads and paths through the Armagnac Country, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily its own distinct loop, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You'll find it crosses small portions of the Voie Verte de l'Armagnac, Voie de Vézelay Est, and the Circuit Mézinais - Ténarèze - 75 km.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: You can expect quintessential Armagnac Country scenery. The route is characterized by rolling country roads passing through vast vineyards and bright yellow sunflower fields. You'll also encounter a rich tapestry of authentic villages, medieval bastides, traditional Gascon mansions, and small churches, offering a glimpse into the region's history and culture.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this loop?
faqs.answer: The primary challenge is the significant elevation gain over 72.8 kilometers, requiring very good fitness. An insider tip is to plan your stops in the charming villages like Fourcès, which is a unique circular bastide, or Courrensan, to break up the ride and enjoy the local atmosphere and history. Ensure your bike is in excellent condition for the climbs.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting points of the loop?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the smaller villages like Courrensan or Fourcès are limited. However, Eauze, being a larger town, may have more regional bus connections. It's generally recommended to arrive by car or cycle to the starting point, as bringing bicycles on public transport in rural areas can be challenging.
